latest.title: Change of name and TIDM
latest.content:
Today 10:49
RNS Number : 5289O
Cykel AI PLC
12 January 2026
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
12 January 2026
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC (formerly Cykel AI PLC)
(the "
Company
")
Change of name and TIDM
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC (LSE: DFDV), a Main Market listed company which offers an AI agent platform that enables businesses to automate tasks through the use of specialised digital workers for
sales, recruitment and research and has adopted
a Solana focused digital asset treasury strategy,
is pleased to announce that the Company has changed its name and will trade under its new name DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC with effect from 12 January 2026.
In addition, with effect from 13 January 2026, the Company's TIDM will be amended from "CYK" to "DFDV" on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange.
The decision to change name to
DeFi Development Corporation UK PLC
reflects the Company's continued development of its Solana focused digital asset treasury strategy as previously announced on 28 August 2025. The "Cykel" trading name will continue to be used for the Company's AI agent activities.
In addition, the Company's website address will also change to www.defidevcorp.co.uk.
Shareholders should note that their shareholdings will be unaffected by the change of name. Existing share certificates should be retained as they will remain valid for all purposes and no new share certificates will be issued.
The Company's ISIN and SEDOL will remain unchanged. The Company's Legal Entity Identifier remains 213800QEO6L6JAS62H02.
The Directors of the Company accept responsibility for this announcement.
*ENDS*
For further information:
Company
Michael Chan
Director

latest.title: Further re warrants
latest.content:
4 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 1455K
Cykel AI PLC
04 December 2025
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
4 December 2025
Cykel AI PLC
("
Cykel AI
" or the "
Company
")
Further re Warrants
Cykel AI PLC (LSE:CYK) announces the following with respect to the warrants, full details of which are set out in the Company's announcement of 28 August 2025.
Warrants cancellation
The Company announces that it has agreed to the cancellation of 5,000,000 Pre-Paid and Investor Warrants (together, the "Warrants"). This will result in the repayment of the deposit already paid for the Pre-Paid Warrants (less a contribution to transaction costs) to the cancelling holders in accordance with the terms of the Warrants. Following this cancellation, the Company has 41,666,667 Warrants outstanding.
Warrants extension details
Under the existing terms, Warrants are not capable of being exercised until certain conditions which are detailed in the 28 August 2025 announcement have been satisfied and would have expired if such conditions were not satisfied by 30 November 2025. The Company and the remaining warrant holders, including DeFi Development Corp, have agreed to extend the period within which these conditions may be satisfied to 1 June 2026. Accordingly, the Warrants will only become exercisable once the relevant conditions have been fulfilled on or before 1 June 2026.
If the conditions have not been satisfied by 1 June 2026, the Warrants will expire and be cancelled and the deposit for the Pre-Paid Warrants will be repaid to the relevant investors (less pre
‑
agreed contributions to transaction costs).
All other terms of the Warrants remain unchanged.
Pre-Paid Warrants and Investor Warrants have the meanings given to them in the announcement published on 28 August 2025.
For further information:
Cykel AI plc
Michael Chan
CEO

latest.title: Changes in Board Composition
latest.content:
3 Nov 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 7828F
Cykel AI PLC
03 November 2025
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended). Upon the publication of this announcement via a Regulatory Information Service, this inside information is now considered to be in the public domain.
3 November 2025
Cykel AI PLC
("Cykel AI" or the "Company")
Changes in Board Composition
Cykel AI PLC (LSE:CYK) announces that Nicholas Lyth has resigned as Interim Chairman of the Company, with immediate effect.
The Board would like to take this opportunity to thank Mr. Lyth for his contributions to the Company and wish him well for the future.
Michael Chan will assume the role of Chairman on an interim basis pending the appointment of a Non-executive Chairman in due course.
For further information:
Cykel AI plc
Michael Chan
CEO
investors@cykel.ai
First Sentinel (Financial Adviser)

company_30s.what_they_do: Cykel AI builds AI‑powered sales tools. Its flagship product, Eve, is an autonomous sales agent that can handle outreach, follow‑ups and deal‑closing, while its GTM AI platform helps businesses launch and scale marketing campaigns automatically.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global sales‑automation market is projected to grow into a multi‑billion‑dollar space, and AI is the main differentiator. Cykel AI claims its solutions cut sales cycles by 40‑60 % and reduce manual effort, giving it a clear niche against legacy CRMs and generic chatbot vendors.
company_30s.current_state: The company is still in the early‑stage funding phase, having raised several rounds to support product development and market entry. It has yet to post sustained revenue, but it has moved from a research‑only phase to live product pilots and a new Bitcoin‑backed treasury strategy aimed at stabilising cash flow.

rns_narrative: Cykel AI has been busy restructuring its corporate identity, bringing in new board members, and raising fresh capital to fuel product roll‑outs. The company announced a 2 million‑pound equity raise in August, followed by a smaller £750k placement in May, showing that investors are willing to keep the engine running.

Parallel to the fundraising, the firm has pushed two major AI releases: the Eve sales agent and the GTM AI platform, both designed to prove the commercial viability of its technology. These launches signal a shift from prototype to production‑grade offerings.

The most recent corporate move is the adoption of a Bitcoin‑treasury reserve strategy, a bold attempt to diversify liquidity and hedge against inflation. While still experimental, it hints at a longer‑term play to keep the company afloat until a clear revenue stream emerges.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Cautious optimism after a series of product launches
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the new AI tools and the Bitcoin reserve as proof that the company is building something valuable. They see the recent funding as validation that investors are willing to back the tech.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears argue the firm is still cash‑burning with no proven revenue and has been on a steep slide. They worry that the Bitcoin strategy is a gimmick and that the company may run out of runway before it can monetize.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter shifted from bleak in early October to more hopeful in early January, coinciding with the product announcements and the Bitcoin reserve news.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat – nobody is searching for it, which is exactly what contrarian traders look for.

near_term_catalysts: The next interim results on 30 Oct 2025 will show whether the new AI tools have started generating revenue or at least sign‑ups. A positive earnings beat or a clear path to profitability will be a trigger.
If the company announces a new partnership or a pilot with a mid‑market firm, that would signal real‑world adoption and could lift the stock.
A spike in daily volume that breaks the 50‑day moving average would indicate renewed investor interest and could be the first sign of a bounce.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden jump in shares traded (e.g., > 500 k shares in a day) and for any mention of a revenue milestone in press releases or social posts. A clear statement that the Bitcoin‑treasury is fully operational would also be a confirmation.
expect_next.timing_regime: The price is still near the bottom of its crash cycle, and the APEX compression score suggests the market is tightening around the current low. If volume and sentiment begin to lift, the mean‑reversion window is likely to open.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company sits at a deep bottom, and the new AI products plus the Bitcoin treasury give it a realistic path to cash flow. If the stock climbs to a price that is only a few ticks above its current level, the upside is several hundred percent, while the downside is capped at the current floor.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest threat is that the product launches fail to generate traction and the company burns through its capital before it can turn a profit. In that scenario the stock could stay flat or slide back to zero.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential gain is roughly 5‑to‑1: a modest loss if the price stays at zero, versus a massive gain if it recovers to the mid‑level of its recent low.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter at the first dip – buy 1 share at the lowest price seen so far. Use a 10‑tick stop below the entry to protect against a false breakout. Scale in with each new volume spike, keeping position size small relative to total capital.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume surge that exceeds 500 k shares or for a clear positive earnings announcement. The entry trigger could be a 3‑tick breakout above the current high after a confirmed volume spike.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces bankruptcy, a delisting, or a clear regulatory issue, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: The stock sits at a deep crash, recent AI launches and a Bitcoin‑treasury strategy are the catalysts, and the upside is dramatically higher than the downside – a classic contrarian play.
